checks.describe: the live model pod exposes a startupProbe whose failureThreshold x periodSeconds is the load budget — read off the running pod (Deep Dive: startupProbe anatomy). Self-seeds the cluster + deploy
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate k8s-up >/dev/null 2>&1; bash labs/m7/seed.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; kubectl get deploy opsmate-model >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl apply -f labs/opsmate/k8s/model-server.yaml >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-model --timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; FT=$(kubectl get pod -l app=opsmate-model -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].startupProbe.failureThreshold}' 2>/dev/null); PS=$(kubectl get pod -l app=opsmate-model -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].startupProbe.periodSeconds}' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FT" ] && [ -n "$PS" ] && [ "$FT" -ge 10 ] 2>/dev/null && [ $((FT*PS)) -ge 90 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo budget-ok; else echo budget-too-tight; fi

checks.describe: under load the engine queues (requests_deferred is exposed) while /health stays green — the Service-backpressure disconnect: readiness is binary, the metric is the graded signal (Deep Dive: Service backpressure). Self-seeds; uses a background port-forward
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-model --timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; ( kubectl port-forward service/opsmate-model 18081:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/m8-dd-pf.pid ); sleep 4; H=$(curl -s --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:18081/health | jq -r .status 2>/dev/null); M=$(curl -s --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:18081/metrics | grep -c '^llamacpp:requests_deferred'); kill "$(cat /tmp/m8-dd-pf.pid 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null; rm -f /tmp/m8-dd-pf.pid; if [ "$H" = "ok" ] && [ "$M" -ge 1 ]; then echo health-green-metric-present; else echo signal-missing; fi

checks.describe: the serving vs batch PriorityClasses apply and carry the right values — serving high (preempts batch), batch low (yields under pressure) — the M11 setup (Deep Dive: PriorityClass). Applies and cleans up its own resources
checks.run:
export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate k8s-up >/dev/null 2>&1; printf 'apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: model-serving
value: 1000000
globalDefault: false
---
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: batch-inference
value: 1000
globalDefault: false
' | kubectl apply -f - >/dev/null 2>&1; SV=$(kubectl get priorityclass model-serving -o jsonpath='{.value}' 2>/dev/null); BV=$(kubectl get priorityclass batch-inference -o jsonpath='{.value}' 2>/dev/null); kubectl delete priorityclass model-serving batch-inference >/dev/null 2>&1; if [ "$SV" = "1000000" ] && [ "$BV" = "1000" ] && [ "$SV" -gt "$BV" ] 2>/dev/null; then echo priority-ok; else echo priority-bad; fi
